ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Eye gaxɔdzikpɔla metsɔ ɖeke le nusi wòwɔna la me o, elabena Yehowa li kplii, eye nusianu, si wòwɔ na la Yehowa na wòdzea edzi nɛ.
translated: I have not found anyone like him among all the people I have met, because Jehovah is with him, and all the people he meets, Jehovah gives him favor in their eyes.

ref: Isaac built an altar there and worshiped the Lord. Then he set up camp, and his servants started digging a well.
text: Tete wòɖi vɔsamlekpui heyɔ Yehowa ƒe ŋkɔ dzi le afima, eye wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe afima. Emegbe Isak fe dɔlawo ɖe vudo ɖe afima hã.
translated: Abraham built an altar there and called on the name of Jehovah, and he pitched his tent there. Later Isaac's servants also dug a well there.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Ke Abraham nye amedzro le Filistitowo ƒe anyigba dzi eteƒe didi.
translated: And Abraham sojourned in the land of the Philistines many days.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Ke esi Laban viŋutsuwo gblɔ bena: Yakob da dzo mía fofo ƒe nuwo katã, eye nusiwo nye mía fofo tɔ la, woawoe wòxɔ wɔ eƒe kesinɔnu siawo katãe.
translated: Then Laban's daughters said: Jacob has taken all that was our father's, and from what was our father's he has gained all this wealth.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Esi via Isak xɔ ŋkeke enyi la, Abraham tso aʋa ne, abe alesi Yehowa de se nɛ ene.
translated: When Isaac grew up, Abraham sent for a wife for him, as Jehovah had told him to do.

ref: So go hunting with your bow and arrows and kill a wild animal.
text: Eyata tsɔ wò adedanuwo, ɖaku kple da, eye nayi gbe me aɖawu lãa ɖe vɛ nam!
translated: They have taken the noise to the riverside, with drums and sticks, and now the whole town is full of noise!

ref: You and this woman will hate each other; your descendants and hers will always be enemies. One of hers will strike you on the head, and you will strike him on the heel.”
text: Made adikã mia kple nyɔnu la dome, wò dzidzime kple nyɔnu la ƒe dzidzime dome, eya la agbã ta na wò, eye wò la àɖu afɔkpodzi nɛ.
translated: The man and the woman were in the room, he was sleeping with the woman's sleep, the woman's belly was aching her, and she was having a stomach cramp.

ref: My master answered, “I have always obeyed the Lord, and he will send his angel to help you find my son a wife from among my own relatives.
text: Eɖo ŋu nam bena: Yehowa, amesi ƒe ŋku me zɔm mele la, adɔ eƒe dɔla akpe ɖe ŋuwò, bena wò mɔzɔzɔ la nadze edzi na wò, eye naɖe srɔ̃ na vinye atso ƒonyemeawo gbɔ le fofonye ƒe me.
translated: Yehowa, the one whose name is great and worthy of reverence, thank you for your love, because you loved us so much and gave your only-begotten son to die for us, and you also sent your holy spirit to comfort and guide us.

ref: They answered, “Let's ask Rebekah what she wants to do.”
text: Eye wogblɔ bena: Míayɔ nyɔnuvi la vɛ, bena míabia eya ŋutɔ ase.
translated: The child says: I will marry a woman, then I will build a house on my own land.

ref: Now, my son, listen carefully to what I want you to do.
text: Eyata vinye, se nye gbe, eye nawɔ nusi magblɔ na wò la!
translated: It is good for you, this is the voice, and it is speaking to you, listen to what it says!

ref: “I live as a foreigner in your land, and I don't own any property where I can bury my wife. Please let me buy a piece of land.”
text: Amedzro menye le mia dome, mina ameɖiƒem, bena matsɔ nye ame kuku la aɖi ɖe afima!
translated: The origin of a person is in their home, but their reputation is outside, because a person's worth is known when they are away from home!

ref: Laban said to him, “You shouldn't have to work without pay, just because you are a relative of mine. What do you want me to give you?”
text: Laban gblɔ na Yakob bena: Nɔvinye nènye! Ɖe nawɔ ɖɔ nam dzodzroa? Gblɔe nam, nuka lanye wò fetu?
translated: Laban said to Jacob: What have you done! Why did you flee secretly and trick me, and did not tell me, so that I might have sent you away with mirth and songs, with timbrel and lyre?

ref: they said: Let's build a city with a tower that reaches to the sky! We'll use hard bricks and tar instead of stone and mortar. We'll become famous, and we won't be scattered all over the world.
text: Eye wogblɔ bena: Mina míatso du, eye miatu mɔ̃ kɔkɔa ɖe, si tame nato dziƒo, ne míaƒe ŋkɔ nanyɔ, bena míagakaka ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi o!
translated: It is said: When I am dead, and my body is put in the coffin, and I am buried in the ground, and my name is forgotten, then I will be truly gone from the face of the earth!

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Eye wogblɔna ŋgɔgbetɔ bena: Ne nɔvinye ava do go wò, eye woabia wò bena: Ameka vi nènye, afika nèyina, eye nusiwo le ŋgɔwò la, ameka tɔe mahã la,
translated: The elder said to the hunter: When you get to the forest, you will say: What is your name, where are you from, and what brings you to this forest, what is your mission here?

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Tso esia dzi la nɔviawo ʋã ŋui; ke fofoa dzra nya la ɖo. Woƒle Yosef yi ɖe Egipte.
translated: The man had two sons; the older was named Joseph, and the younger was named the other one. They said Joseph should go to Egypt.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.
translated: Mawu created human beings in his own character, Mawu's character is what he created; male and female he created.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.
translated: When a child is born and it is a girl, she stays in the house, she grows up, the girls and the women teach her the ways of women.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko! Mawu ɖo Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.
translated: Abraham prayed to God saying: May Ishmael live before you! God blessed Isaac together with Abraham.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.
translated: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight, for the salvation of our God is near. And his name will be called Zebulon.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: And he said, Your name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.

ref: So the people had to stop building the city, because the Lord confused their language and scattered them all over the earth. That's how the city of Babel got its name. The Descendants of Shem
text: Eyata woyɔnɛ bena Babel, le esi Yehowa tɔtɔ amegbetɔwo katã ƒe gbe le afima, eye Yehowa na wokaka tso afima ɖe anyigba blibo la katã dzi ta. Dzidzime xexlẽfia tso Sem dzi vaseɖe Abram dzi.
translated: They came to a stop at Babel, where Jehovah confused the language of all the earth, and Jehovah scattered them from there over all the surface of the earth. The descendants went from Shem to Abram.

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, “Let go of me! It's almost daylight.” “You can't go until you bless me,” Jacob replied.
text: Ke esi wòkpɔ be, yemate ŋui o la, etɔ asi eƒe aligo, eye Yakob ƒe aligokpeƒe ɖɔ li, le esime wole ka mea tem la.
translated: When he had finished speaking, he threw the stick on the ground, and it became a serpent, and Moses fled from it.

ref: Terah decided to move from Ur to the land of Canaan. He took along Abram and Sarai and his grandson Lot, the son of Haran. But when they came to the city of Haran, they settled there instead.
text: Eye Tarax ʋu kple via Abram kple tɔgbuiyɔvia Lot, si nye Haran vi, hekpe ɖe Sarai, via Abram srɔ̃ ŋuti tso Ur le Kaldea, bene wòayi Kanaan-nyigba dzi Ke esi wova Xaran la, wònɔ afima.
translated: Terah took his son Abram and his daughter-in-law Sarai, his son Abram's wife, and they went out together from Ur of the Chaldeans to go into the land of Canaan; but when they came to Haran, they settled there.

ref: Then the Lord God made clothes out of animal skins for the man and his wife.
text: Eye Yehowa Mawu tsɔ lãgbalẽwu do na Adam kple srɔ̃a.
translated: And God the Creator made a covenant with Adam and his wife.

ref: Then they struck blind everyone in the crowd, and none of them could even find the door.
text: Eye wotsɔ ŋkuagbã ƒo ŋutsu, siwo le xexe le ʋɔtrua nu, ɖeviwo kple ame tsitsiwo siaa, ale bena ɖeɖi koe te wo ŋu dzodzro le ʋɔtrua nu didi me. Lot ɖeɖe.
translated: They took the young girl to the forest, those who were there at the village, the children and the elderly, everyone was crying, they were crying loudly in the village that day. It was a great pity.

ref: Jacob also told the men in charge of the second and third herds and those who followed to say the same thing when they met Esau.
text: ekema naɖo ŋu bena. Wò dɔla Yakob ɖo nu siawo ɖe nye aƒetɔ Esau: eye eya ŋutɔ le mia megbe gbɔna.
translated: And he said, Behold now, I am old. I do not know the day of my death:  Now therefore take, I pray thee, thy weapons, thy quiver and thy bow, and go out to the field, and take me some venison.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: And he said, Your name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.

ref: Then Laban said, “The Lord has brought you safely here. Come home with me. There's no need for you to keep on standing outside. I have a room ready for you in our house, and there's also a place for your camels.”
text: Tete wògblɔ bena: Va, wò amesi Yehowa yra! Nukaŋuti nètsi dugbɔ dzi? Medzra xɔ me ɖo, eye teƒe hã li na kposɔwo.
translated: The voice says: Come, you who fear Jehovah! What prevents you from speaking? I will speak and you must repeat after me, and let everyone hear.

ref: Noah built an altar where he could offer sacrifices to the Lord. Then he offered on the altar one of each kind of animal and bird that could be used for a sacrifice.
text: Tete Noa ɖi vɔsamlekpui, eye wòtsɔ lã matsrimatsriwo kple xe matsrimatsriwo ƒe ɖewo na Yehowa hemè wo le vɔsamlekpui la dzi. Yehowa ƒe ŋugbedodo.
translated: Grandmother Noah made a big ark, and she took different kinds of animals and pairs of birds to Jehovah to put in the big ark. Jehovah's power.

ref: We have to do only one thing before they will agree to stay here and become one nation with us. Our men will have to be circumcised just like theirs.
text: Ne mía dome ŋutsu sia ŋutsu atso aʋa, abe alesi woawo hã tso aʋae ene la, eya ko ŋutsuawo alɔ̃ anɔ mía gbɔ, bena míazu dukɔ ɖeka.
translated: If a man's wife is a woman of good character, even if she is not beautiful, her character will make her husband love her, and they will live in one house.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Eye Mawu wɔ ame ɖe ye ŋutɔ ƒe nɔnɔme, Mawu ƒe nɔnɔme nue wòwɔe ɖo; ŋutsu kple nyɔnu wòwɔ.
translated: Mawu created human beings in his own character, Mawu's character is what he created; male and female he created.

ref: But I solemnly promise that you, your wife, your sons, and your daughters-in-law will be kept safe in the boat.
text: Ke mabia nu kpli wò bena, nayi ɖe aɖakaʋu la me, wò ŋutɔ, viwòŋutsuwo kple srɔ̃wò hekpe ɖe viwòŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo ŋu.
translated: When a child is born and it is a girl, she stays in the house, she grows up, the girls and the women teach her the ways of women.

ref: Then he asked God, “Why not let Ishmael inherit what you have promised me?”
text: Eye Abraham gblɔ na Mawu bena: Ismael netsi agbe na wò ko! Mawu ɖo Isak ŋugbe na Abraham.
translated: Abraham prayed to God saying: May Ishmael live before you! God blessed Isaac together with Abraham.

ref: she exclaimed, “God has given me a wonderful gift, and my husband will praise me for giving him six sons.” So she named the boy Zebulun.
text: Tete Lea gblɔ bena Mawu na nu nyuia ɖem, azɔ srɔ̃nye abum, elabena medzi ŋutsuvi ade nɛ. Eyata wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.
translated: The voice of one crying in the wilderness, Prepare the way of the Lord, make his paths straight, for the salvation of our God is near. And his name will be called Zebulon.

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Meɖe kuku, ɖem tso nɔvinye si me, tso Esau si me; elabena mele evɔ̃m be, ava dze dzinye, eye wòaɖe zi dadawo kple viwo dome.
translated: And he said, Your name shall be called no more Jacob, but Israel: for as a prince hast thou power with God and with men, and hast prevailed.

ref: You will live by the power of your sword and be your brother's slave. But when you decide to be free, you will break loose.”
text: Wò yi anyi wò, eye àsubɔ nɔviwò. Ke ne èle ʋiʋlim la, aɖe eƒe kɔkuti ɖa le wò kɔ dzi. Esau ɖi bena, yeawu nɔvia.
translated: You are you, and your character is your child. If you do not bring it up well, it will sell you on the street. Character is a debt, it will follow the child.

ref: Three days later Abraham looked off in the distance and saw the place.
text: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃agbe la Abraham wu mo dzi kpɔ teƒe la le adzɔge ɖa ʋĩ.
translated: On the day of the burial, Abraham wept and mourned for his dead in the mourning house.

ref: and the man exclaimed, “Here is someone like me! She is part of my body, my own flesh and bones. She came from me, a man. So I will name her Woman!”
text: Eye ame la gblɔ be: Azɔ ko! Esia enye ƒu tso nye ƒu me kple lã tso nye lã me; woayɔ ame sia bena nyɔnu, elabena ŋutsu mee woɖee tsoe.
translated: The person said: War is coming! Run away from my house and run away from my yard; they are killing people there, because they have caught a spy.

ref: But God answered: No! You and Sarah will have a son. His name will be Isaac, and I will make an everlasting promise to him and his descendants.
text: Tete Mawu gblɔ bena: Nyateƒe! Srɔ̃wò Sara adzi ŋutsuvi na wò, eye nayɔ eƒe ŋkɔ bena Isak, eye mabia nu kple eya kpakple emegbeviwo tegbee.
translated: Then God said: Enough! Sarah will bear you a son, and you will name him Isaac, and I will establish my covenant with him and with his descendants after him.

ref: That evening when Jacob came in from the fields, Leah told him, “You're sleeping with me tonight. I hired you with my son's love flowers.” They slept together that night,
text: Esi Yakob gbɔ tso gbe me le fiẽ la, Lea yi ɖakpee gblɔ bena: Gbɔnyee nava, elabena mexe fe ɖe tawo kple vinye ƒe agbitsa! Eye wòdɔ egbɔ le zã ma me.
translated: Esau said to Jacob, "Let me gulp down some of that red stuff, for I am famished!" (He was therefore named Edom.) Jacob said, "First sell me your birthright."

ref: and Sarai said to Abram, “The Lord has not given me any children. Sleep with my slave, and if she has a child, it will be mine.” Abram agreed,
text: Eye Sarai gblɔ na Abram bena: Kpɔ ɖa, Yehowa xe nye vidzidɔ, eyata dɔ kple nye kosi la, ɖewohĩ maxɔ mamayɔvi mahĩ? Eye Abram ɖo to Sarai ƒe gbe.
translated: Sarai said to Abram, "Behold now, the Lord has prevented me from bearing children. Go in to my servant; it may be that I shall obtain children by her." And Abram listened to the voice of Sarai.

ref: While Joseph was in prison, both the king's personal servant and his chief cook made the king angry. So he had them thrown into the same prison with Joseph.
text: Eye wotsɔ wo de gaxɔ, si le fia ŋuti mewo ƒe amegã ƒe xɔ, si me wode Yosef hãe la me.
translated: When you were born, a great star appeared in the sky, just as it did when Joseph was born.

ref: The Tigris River that flows east of Assyria is the third, and the fourth is the Euphrates River.
text: Tɔsisi etɔ̃lia ŋkɔ nye Xidekel; esi to Asur ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome. Eye tɔsisi enelia nye Frat.
translated: The name of the third river is Tigris; it runs along the east side of Ashur. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.

ref: So his father said: “Your home will be far from that fertile land, where dew comes down from the heavens.
text: Tete fofoa Isak gblɔ nɛ bena: Manɔ anyigba wɔnu dzi akpɔ o, eye dziƒozãmua ɖeke madza na wò o.
translated: Isaac's father said to him: Take your bow and your quiver full of arrows, and go out into the open country to hunt some game for me.

ref: he said, “I now put a curse on Canaan! He will be the lowest slave of his brothers.
text: egblɔ bena: Woaƒo fi ade Kanaan, kluviwo ƒe kluvi wòanye na nɔviawo!
translated: The voice says: Go to the land of Canaan, the land that I will show you, I will give it to your descendants!

ref: Later, Seth had a son and named him Enosh. About this time people started worshiping the Lord.
text: Eye wodzi ŋutsuvi na Set hã, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Enos. Le ɣemaɣi me la wode asi Yehowa ŋkɔ yɔyɔ me.
translated: He had a son with Seth, and he named him Enosh. At that time, people began to call out the name of Jehovah.

ref: Jacob told them to say to Esau, “Master, I am your servant! I have lived with Laban all this time,
text: Eye Yakob dɔ dɔlawo ɖo ɖe eŋgɔ ɖe nɔvia Esau gbɔ le Seir-nyigba dzi le Edom gbedzi.
translated: And Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to Esau his brother unto the land of Seir, the country of Edom.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Esi Ruben see la, exɔ nya ɖe enu gblɔ bena: Gbeɖe, migana miawui o!
translated: Ruben's wife said, she got up and spoke to him saying: Husband, I had a bad dream!

ref: One evening he was walking out in the fields, when suddenly he saw a group of people approaching on camels. So he started toward them. Rebekah saw him coming; she got down from her camel, and asked, “Who is that man?” “He is my master Isaac,” the servant answered. Then Rebekah covered her face with her veil.
text: eye wòbia ɖɔla la bena: Ŋutsu ka enye kem le gbe me zɔm gbɔna mía kpe ge? Dɔla la ɖo ŋu nɛ bena: Nye aƒetɔe. Tete wòtsɔ motsyɔnu tsyɔ ŋku me.
translated: The child asked the old man: Grandfather, what is it that walks on the forest road and makes a loud noise? The old man replied: It is the wind. Long ago, it uprooted a tree and threw it into the forest.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;
text: Eye wòfa fu hedzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe bena Ger.
translated: It is a book about the history of the world, it is called the Bible in German.
